history.text: Help me prioritize my renovation projects

To prioritize your renovation projects effectively, start by identifying which tasks are critical to making your home safe or habitable, such as fixing roof leaks, repairing faulty wiring, or addressing structural issues, as these should always take precedence. Next, consider your budget and cash flow; allocate funds to high-impact projects that add the most value or solve immediate problems before tackling cosmetic updates like painting or flooring. Evaluate your timeline as well, scheduling labor-intensive work early in the calendar to allow sufficient time for drying or settling, and save smaller, quicker jobs for later. Finally, list all projects, assign a rough cost and difficulty level to each, and rank them based on a combination of urgency, budget availability, and personal desire, adjusting the plan as new quotes or constraints emerge.
